assigned.descriptionHtml: An issue was assigned to a user.
assigned.summaryHtml:
This event occurs when there is activity relating to an issue. For more information about issues, see "About issues." For information about the APIs to manage issues, see the GraphQL documentation or "Issues" in the REST API documentation.

For activity relating to a comment on an issue, use the issue_comment event.

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Issues" repository permission.

bodyParameters.name: action
bodyParameters.description: The action that was performed.

bodyParameters.name: enterprise
bodyParameters.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Webhook payloads contain the enterprise property when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account. For more information, see "About enterprise accounts."

bodyParameters.name: installation
bodyParameters.description: The GitHub App installation. Webhook payloads contain the installation property when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App. For more information, see "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps."

bodyParameters.name: issue
bodyParameters.description: The issue itself.

childParamsGroups.name: author_association
childParamsGroups.description: How the author is associated with the repository.

childParamsGroups.name: color
childParamsGroups.description: 6-character hex code, without the leading #, identifying the color

childParamsGroups.name: milestone
childParamsGroups.description: A collection of related issues and pull requests.

childParamsGroups.name: performed_via_github_app
childParamsGroups.description: GitHub apps are a new way to extend GitHub. They can be installed directly on organizations and user accounts and granted access to specific repositories. They come with granular permissions and built-in webhooks. GitHub apps are first class actors within GitHub.

childParamsGroups.name: events
childParamsGroups.description: The list of events for the GitHub app Supported values are: branch_protection_rule, check_run, check_suite, code_scanning_alert, commit_comment, content_reference, create, delete, deployment, deployment_review, deployment_status, deploy_key, discussion, discussion_comment, fork, gollum, issues, issue_comment, label, member, membership, milestone, organization, org_block, page_build, project, project_card, project_column, public, pull_request, pull_request_review, pull_request_review_comment, push, registry_package, release, repository, repository_dispatch, secret_scanning_alert, star, status, team, team_add, watch, workflow_dispatch, workflow_run, reminder, pull_request_review_thread

childParamsGroups.name: permissions
childParamsGroups.description: The set of permissions for the GitHub app

childParamsGroups.name: slug
childParamsGroups.description: The slug name of the GitHub app

childParamsGroups.name: pinned_comment
childParamsGroups.description: Comments provide a way for people to collaborate on an issue.

childParamsGroups.name: author_association
childParamsGroups.description: How the author is associated with the repository.

childParamsGroups.name: pin
childParamsGroups.description: Context around who pinned an issue comment and when it was pinned.

childParamsGroups.name: data_type
childParamsGroups.description: The data type of the issue field

childParamsGroups.type: null or string or number or integer
childParamsGroups.name: value
single_select_option
childParamsGroups.description: Details about the selected option (only present for single_select fields)

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: The unique identifier of the issue type.

childParamsGroups.name: node_id
childParamsGroups.description: The node identifier of the issue type.

childParamsGroups.name: updated_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time the issue type last updated.

childParamsGroups.name: is_enabled
childParamsGroups.description: The enabled state of the issue type.

bodyParameters.name: organization
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub organization. Webhook payloads contain the organization property when the webhook is configured for an organization, or when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization.

bodyParameters.name: repository
bodyParameters.description: The repository on GitHub where the event occurred. Webhook payloads contain the repository property when the event occurs from activity in a repository.

bodyParameters.name: sender
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub user.

pinned.descriptionHtml: An issue was pinned to a repository. For more information, see "Pinning an issue to your repository."
pinned.summaryHtml:
This event occurs when there is activity relating to an issue. For more information about issues, see "About issues." For information about the APIs to manage issues, see the GraphQL documentation or "Issues" in the REST API documentation.

For activity relating to a comment on an issue, use the issue_comment event.

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Issues" repository permission.

bodyParameters.name: enterprise
bodyParameters.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Webhook payloads contain the enterprise property when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account. For more information, see "About enterprise accounts."

bodyParameters.name: installation
bodyParameters.description: The GitHub App installation. Webhook payloads contain the installation property when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App. For more information, see "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps."

bodyParameters.name: issue
bodyParameters.description: The issue itself.

unlocked.descriptionHtml: Conversation on an issue was locked. For more information, see "Locking conversations."
unlocked.summaryHtml:
This event occurs when there is activity relating to an issue. For more information about issues, see "About issues." For information about the APIs to manage issues, see the GraphQL documentation or "Issues" in the REST API documentation.

For activity relating to a comment on an issue, use the issue_comment event.

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Issues" repository permission.

bodyParameters.name: enterprise
bodyParameters.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Webhook payloads contain the enterprise property when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account. For more information, see "About enterprise accounts."

bodyParameters.name: installation
bodyParameters.description: The GitHub App installation. Webhook payloads contain the installation property when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App. For more information, see "Using webhooks with GitHub Apps."

bodyParameters.name: issue
bodyParameters.description: The issue itself.

childParamsGroups.name: author_association
childParamsGroups.description: How the author is associated with the repository.

childParamsGroups.name: milestone
childParamsGroups.description: A collection of related issues and pull requests.

childParamsGroups.name: performed_via_github_app
childParamsGroups.description: GitHub apps are a new way to extend GitHub. They can be installed directly on organizations and user accounts and granted access to specific repositories. They come with granular permissions and built-in webhooks. GitHub apps are first class actors within GitHub.

childParamsGroups.name: events
childParamsGroups.description: The list of events for the GitHub app Supported values are: branch_protection_rule, check_run, check_suite, code_scanning_alert, commit_comment, content_reference, create, delete, deployment, deployment_review, deployment_status, deploy_key, discussion, discussion_comment, fork, gollum, issues, issue_comment, label, member, membership, milestone, organization, org_block, page_build, project, project_card, project_column, public, pull_request, pull_request_review, pull_request_review_comment, push, registry_package, release, repository, repository_dispatch, secret_scanning_alert, star, status, team, team_add, watch, workflow_dispatch, workflow_run

childParamsGroups.name: permissions
childParamsGroups.description: The set of permissions for the GitHub app

childParamsGroups.name: slug
childParamsGroups.description: The slug name of the GitHub app

childParamsGroups.name: pinned_comment
childParamsGroups.description: Comments provide a way for people to collaborate on an issue.

childParamsGroups.name: author_association
childParamsGroups.description: How the author is associated with the repository.

childParamsGroups.name: pin
childParamsGroups.description: Context around who pinned an issue comment and when it was pinned.

childParamsGroups.name: data_type
childParamsGroups.description: The data type of the issue field

childParamsGroups.type: null or string or number or integer
childParamsGroups.name: value
single_select_option
childParamsGroups.description: Details about the selected option (only present for single_select fields)

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: The unique identifier of the issue type.

childParamsGroups.name: node_id
childParamsGroups.description: The node identifier of the issue type.

childParamsGroups.name: updated_at
childParamsGroups.description: The time the issue type last updated.

childParamsGroups.name: is_enabled
childParamsGroups.description: The enabled state of the issue type.

bodyParameters.name: organization
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub organization. Webhook payloads contain the organization property when the webhook is configured for an organization, or when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization.

bodyParameters.name: repository
bodyParameters.description: The repository on GitHub where the event occurred. Webhook payloads contain the repository property when the event occurs from activity in a repository.

bodyParameters.name: sender
bodyParameters.description: A GitHub user.

unpinned.descriptionHtml: An issue was unpinned from a repository. For more information, see "Pinning an issue to your repository."
unpinned.summaryHtml:

To subscribe to this event, a GitHub App must have at least read-level access for the "Issues" repository permission.
